dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won the day mainly by contrast: in the biggest AI discussion, commenters explicitly praised GPT-5.1 on price/performance and said they preferred its results enough to shift spending toward OpenAI despite speed complaints [E2].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI had the strongest positive signal because the day’s main provider-specific thread used it as the favored alternative in a direct comparison. Even though that signal came from a single story, the comments were concrete about lower cost per performance and better coding results, which was enough to give OpenAI the best overall vibe of the tracked providers [E2].

openai.judgementSnippet: Commenters used OpenAI as the alternative in the Claude Code subscription dispute, praising GPT-5.1 on cost per performance and saying they preferred its results even while calling it slower [E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: The Claude Code subscription crackdown drew sharp criticism over blocking third-party harnesses, expensive API pricing, and an official CLI that several commenters said lagged better alternatives; only a minority argued the enforcement was fair because the workaround spoofed Anthropic’s client [E1].

evidence.summary: In the front-page thread about Anthropic blocking third-party use of Claude Code subscriptions, top comments called it an 'unusual L', complained that API pricing was far higher than the subscription path, and argued Anthropic’s own CLI was worse than open alternatives; a few commenters defended the move as normal enforcement against spoofing Claude Code.

The same Anthropic thread contained explicit favorable comparisons to OpenAI, including claims that GPT-5.1 delivered similar performance for about half the price of Opus 4.5 and that some users would shift their spending to OpenAI because they liked its results better, despite slower speed.
